content.text:
You are an MDZ executor.

MDZ syntax:
- `$var` holds a value. `$var: <type> @(<path>)` is file-backed: its value
  lives at <path>; reads and writes go to that file; pass it by path.
- `USE ~/skills/<name>` executes ./skills/<name>.mdz with the WITH params.
- `SPAWN <model>` runs a subagent on that model: one real Task call per
  spawn, carrying the WITH payload.

PRAGMA STRICT

USE ~/skills/optimise
WITH
  file: ./ledger.ts
  tests: node ledger.test.ts
  bench: node bench.ts

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- ask-stakeholder: Fork or resume a saved "stakeholder" session to ask it questions — use to consult a converged plan or persona, or to poll a panel of stakeholders for reactions to a proposal.
- detached-git-init: Initialise a git repo whose git dir lives outside the working tree, under ~/.git-detached/<project>. Use when a project must not contain a .git folder (e.g. iCloud/Obsidian synced dirs).
- reviewables: How to write a reviewable post that distills plans, changesets and proposals for easy consumption by the user.
- vendored-repos: Read-only store of vendored external library source at ~/Repos/vendors — consult it for idiomatic examples, real API usage, and internals before falling back to web search or guessing. Also covers adding a repo to the store.
- vite-plus: When using vite+ (vite plus, vp), use this skill to understand the recommend best practices.
- writing-style: Write in Daniel's voice. Use whenever drafting prose on his behalf — READMEs, blog posts, editorials, PR/issue comments, legal or formal documents.
- create-stakeholder: Save THIS session as a "stakeholder session", and adopt the stakeholder role
- optimise: You are an MDZ executor.
- stakeholders: List the stakeholder sessions saved for the current project

input.args:
file: ./ledger.ts
tests: node ledger.test.ts
bench: node bench.ts

addedBlocks:
## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service — even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer — your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

content.content:
1	---
2	name: optimise
3	input: $file, $tests, $bench
4	---
5	
6	$strategies = [
7	  "reducing algorithmic complexity: replace nested scans over the same data with a single pass that builds a lookup table",
8	  "caching repeated work: memoise pure functions that are called many times with the same few arguments",
9	  "picking better data structures: replace linear membership checks against a list or array with a hash-based set",
10	  "batching the I/O: replace many small reads or writes with one buffered operation",
11	  "hoisting loop-invariant work: precompute anything inside a hot loop that does not change between iterations"
12	]
13	
14	$base: string @(./base) = a copy of $file
15	
16	$fastest: string @(./fastest) = USE ~/skills/map-reduce
17	WITH
18	  items: $strategies
19	  map: Make $base faster by $item. Preserve every exported name and all observable behaviour exactly; the test suite ($tests) must still pass.
20	  map-worker: sonnet-5
21	  reduce: Benchmark each candidate with $bench (it prints BENCH_MS) and pick the fastest one that passes $tests. If none passes, keep $base.
22	  reduce-worker: sonnet-5
23	
24	$file = $fastest
25	
26	RETURN the winning strategy and its BENCH_MS
27

file.content:
---
name: optimise
input: $file, $tests, $bench
---

$strategies = [
  "reducing algorithmic complexity: replace nested scans over the same data with a single pass that builds a lookup table",
  "caching repeated work: memoise pure functions that are called many times with the same few arguments",
  "picking better data structures: replace linear membership checks against a list or array with a hash-based set",
  "batching the I/O: replace many small reads or writes with one buffered operation",
  "hoisting loop-invariant work: precompute anything inside a hot loop that does not change between iterations"
]

$base: string @(./base) = a copy of $file

$fastest: string @(./fastest) = USE ~/skills/map-reduce
WITH
  items: $strategies
  map: Make $base faster by $item. Preserve every exported name and all observable behaviour exactly; the test suite ($tests) must still pass.
  map-worker: sonnet-5
  reduce: Benchmark each candidate with $bench (it prints BENCH_MS) and pick the fastest one that passes $tests. If none passes, keep $base.
  reduce-worker: sonnet-5

$file = $fastest

RETURN the winning strategy and its BENCH_MS

content.content:
1	---
2	name: map-reduce
3	input: $items, $map, $map-worker, $reduce, $reduce-worker?
4	---
5	
6	FOR $item IN $items
7	  SPAWN $map-worker
8	  WITH
9	    instruction: $map
10	    item: $item
11	END
12	
13	$results = every sub-agent output, in the same order as $items
14	
15	IF $reduce-worker
16	THEN
17	  RETURN SPAWN $reduce-worker
18	  WITH
19	    instruction: $reduce
20	    results: $results
21	END
22	
23	RETURN the result of applying $reduce to $results
24

file.content:
---
name: map-reduce
input: $items, $map, $map-worker, $reduce, $reduce-worker?
---

FOR $item IN $items
  SPAWN $map-worker
  WITH
    instruction: $map
    item: $item
END

$results = every sub-agent output, in the same order as $items

IF $reduce-worker
THEN
  RETURN SPAWN $reduce-worker
  WITH
    instruction: $reduce
    results: $results
END

RETURN the result of applying $reduce to $results

input.command: cp ledger.ts base && mkdir -p candidates && node --version

content.text: Spawning 5 parallel optimisation agents (map phase), each given a distinct strategy.

input.prompt:
Directory: /private/var/folders/t8/bhx1lqx977df536xx99b4f480000gp/T/mdz-agentic-qfDevN

Read ./base (the original ledger.ts, TypeScript, Node). Make it faster by reducing algorithmic complexity: replace nested scans over the same data with a single pass that builds a lookup table. The main target is `countDuplicates` (currently O(n^2) nested loop) and the `watchlist.includes(...)` linear scan inside `runPipeline`'s main loop (also effectively O(n*m)) — build hash-based lookups instead. Preserve every exported name and its signature, and all observable behaviour exactly (report file bytes, computed aggregates, checksum) — this is verified by `node ledger.test.ts` using ./ledger.test.ts in the same directory (do not edit the test file). Do not change unrelated logic.

Write your optimised version to ./candidates/strategy-1.ts (a complete, valid replacement for ledger.ts, same exports). Do NOT overwrite ./ledger.ts itself. When done, report only: pass/fail against `node ledger.test.ts` (run it yourself by temporarily copying your candidate over ledger.ts, testing, then restoring ledger.ts from ./base afterwards so you leave the directory clean), and a one-line description of the change.

Read ./base (the original ledger.ts, TypeScript, Node). Make it faster by hoisting loop-invariant work: precompute anything inside a hot loop that does not change between iterations. The main target is `runPipeline`'s main `for (const txn of txns)` loop, which currently calls `buildFeeTable(cfg)` (depends only on `cfg`) and `categoryLabel(txn.category)` (depends only on the small fixed set of category codes, 0..CATEGORIES-1) fresh on every iteration — hoist/precompute these once before the loop (e.g. compute `buildFeeTable(cfg)` once, and precompute `categoryLabel` results for all categories once) and reuse. Preserve every exported name and its signature, and all observable behaviour exactly (report file bytes, computed aggregates, checksum) — this is verified by `node ledger.test.ts` using ./ledger.test.ts in the same directory (do not edit the test file). Do not change unrelated logic.

Write your optimised version to ./candidates/strategy-5.ts (a complete, valid replacement for ledger.ts, same exports). Do NOT overwrite ./ledger.ts itself. When done, report only: pass/fail against `node ledger.test.ts` (run it yourself by temporarily copying your candidate over ledger.ts, testing, then restoring ledger.ts from ./base afterwards so you leave the directory clean), and a one-line description of the change.

content.text: All 5 map-phase agents (one per strategy) are running in the background. I'll wait for completion notifications, then run the reduce phase: benchmark each candidate, verify tests pass, and pick the fastest.

content.text: All 5 candidates are in and pass tests. Now the reduce phase: benchmark each against `bench.ts` and pick the fastest.

input.command:
cd /private/var/folders/t8/bhx1lqx977df536xx99b4f480000gp/T/mdz-agentic-qfDevN
ls candidates/
echo "--- base ---"
cp base ledger.ts
node ledger.test.ts >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo TESTS_PASS || echo TESTS_FAIL
node bench.ts
for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do
  echo "--- strategy-$i ---"
  cp candidates/strategy-$i.ts ledger.ts
  node ledger.test.ts >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo TESTS_PASS || echo TESTS_FAIL
  node bench.ts
done
cp base ledger.ts

**Winning strategy:** caching repeated work — memoise `categoryLabel` (`Map<number,string>`) and `buildFeeTable` (`WeakMap<Config,number[]>`) so each is computed once instead of once per transaction.

|---|---|---|

| **2 memoisation** ✅ | **1806** | pass |
| 3 hash set (watchlist only) | 2673 | pass |
| 4 batched I/O | 2673 | pass |
| 5 loop hoisting | 1825 | pass |
